explanations.8: This sets up SSH to use a specific private key file and tells SSH not to verify the server's identity by redirecting known hosts checks to /dev/null (useful in containers where you don't have a normal SSH setup).
explanations.10: This creates a bash history file before the container starts so your command history persists between sessions, and tells git to ignore that history file.
explanations.11: This checks if pre-commit (a code quality tool) exists in the container, and if so, installs its git hooks; the 'true' at the end means 'don't fail if pre-commit isn't installed'.
explanations.27: This tells the editor to wrap long JSON comments at a specific column width (80 characters) rather than letting them run off the screen.
explanations.40: This configures the spell checker to understand words in English, French, and Dutch so it doesn't flag them as misspellings.
explanations.53: This makes the keyboard shortcut for multi-cursor selection use Ctrl+Click on Windows/Linux or Cmd+Click on Mac (instead of the default Alt+Click).
explanations.55: These numbers tell the editor to show vertical guide lines at the 120 and 70 character positions so you can see when lines get too long.
explanations.59: This keeps the current scope (function or class name) visible at the top of the editor as you scroll down through code.
explanations.69: This tells VS Code to treat JSON files inside a 'resources' folder as JSONC (JSON with Comments), which allows comments in those files.
explanations.72: This makes the editor automatically switch its language mode to match whatever file is currently active, so you get the right syntax highlighting instantly.
explanations.86: These are regex patterns (a way to match text using special symbols) that tell a code folding extension where 'region' markers start and end in Docker files.
explanations.109: This tells VS Code to validate certain YAML files against official schema definitions online, so you get error checking and autocomplete for those file formats.
